key_strengths: Near-monopoly credit scoring toll-booth with ~88% Scores segment operating margins and ROIC of 48–54%
Asset-light royalty economics: $770M FCF on $8.9M capex, 118% FCF-to-net-income conversion
FICO Score embedded in 90%+ of US lending decisions with high institutional switching costs across auto, card, and personal loan markets
14-year CEO with proven capital allocation discipline: 48% share count reduction since 2010
FICO 10T adoption program with 40+ lenders covering $264B in originations — potential moat re-reinforcement vehicle
March 2026 refinancing extends all debt maturities to 2033–2034, eliminating near-term balance sheet risk
key_risks: FHFA 'Lender Choice' model (July 2025) formally ends de facto GSE mortgage monopoly — the most consequential structural change in FICO's history
Bureau-owned VantageScore priced at $0.99/score (vs. FICO's multi-dollar fees), offered free in FICO bundles — predatory pricing from a counterparty with a direct conflict of interest
Mortgage originations (55% of B2B Scores revenue) subject to triple-threat: rate cycle volume pressure, VantageScore market share gains, and regulatory pricing discipline
Net debt $2.94B (3.3x EBITDA) with leverage amplifying equity downside in any Scores revenue impairment scenario
Software platform transition slow: FICO Platform ARR 32% of total software ARR, total software ARR grew only 4% in FY2025
red_flags: FHFA lender choice model live — de facto government mandate that protected FICO pricing is formally removed
Fannie Mae eliminated minimum FICO score requirement (November 2025) — reduces mandatory score pulls per origination
Bureau-sponsored VantageScore 4.0 at $0.99 from same parties who supply FICO's data infrastructure — dangerous vertical conflict
FICO raised prices 1,600%+ since 2022, generating political and competitive blowback now materializing in coordinated bureau pricing action
CEO sold ~$32M in personal shares over 6 months while board authorized $1.5B in corporate buybacks during live competitive inflection
Negative shareholder equity (-$1.75B) and $2.84B gross debt; no safety cushion for sustained revenue shock
Probability-weighted intrinsic value (~$1,320) is at or below current price ($1,285); unfavorable asymmetry
buy_triggers: Stock declines to $950–1,050, providing genuine 25–30% discount to base-case intrinsic value ($1,350–1,500)
FICO 10T formally adopted by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac as required/preferred standard, re-establishing regulatory embedding
Verifiable FHFA quarterly data shows minimal lender switching to VantageScore after 12+ months of lender choice availability
sell_triggers: Scores revenue growth turns negative for two consecutive quarters, confirming VantageScore share gains outpace pricing power
FHFA or Fannie Mae formally expands score-agnostic underwriting across all agency lending categories
VantageScore captures verifiably more than 25–30% of conforming mortgage origination volume within three years of lender choice implementation
valuation_summary: Base-case DCF intrinsic value of $1,350–1,500/share (using 9% discount rate, 11% FCF CAGR, 25x terminal multiple) implies only 5–17% upside from $1,285; probability-weighted value across bear ($650–750, 25% weight), base (55%), and bull ($2,200–2,500, 20% weight) scenarios is approximately $1,320 — at or slightly below current price, with clearly unfavorable asymmetry.
source_summary: Analysis based on FICO FY2025 10-K (filed November 2025), Q4 2025 earnings release, March 2026 refinancing press releases, and web searches current to March 12–13, 2026; all financial data is fiscal year 2025 or more recent.
